**Abel Enrique Aguilar Tapias** (born 6 January 1985) is a Colombian former professional [footballer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") who played as a [central](/wiki/Midfielder#Central_midfielder "Midfielder") or [defensive midfielder](/wiki/Midfielder#Defensive_midfielder "Midfielder").

Having started his career at [Deportivo Cali](/wiki/Deportivo_Cali "Deportivo Cali"), Aguilar signed with Italian club [Udinese](/wiki/Udinese_Calcio "Udinese Calcio") at the age of 20 then spent seven seasons in [Spanish football](/wiki/Football_in_Spain "Football in Spain") with four teams, appearing in [La Liga](/wiki/La_Liga "La Liga") for [Zaragoza](/wiki/Real_Zaragoza "Real Zaragoza"), [Hércules](/wiki/H%C3%A9rcules_CF "Hércules CF") and [Deportivo](/wiki/Deportivo_de_La_Coru%C3%B1a "Deportivo de La Coruña"). He also competed in France, Portugal and the United States.

A [Colombian international](/wiki/Colombia_national_football_team "Colombia national football team") since 2004, Aguilar represented the nation in two [World Cups](/wiki/FIFA_World_Cup "FIFA World Cup") and as many [Copa América](/wiki/Copa_Am%C3%A9rica "Copa América") tournaments while playing 71 matches.

## Club career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Abel_Aguilar&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Club career")]

### Italy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Abel_Aguilar&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Italy")]

Aguilar was born in [Bogotá](/wiki/Bogot%C3%A1 "Bogotá"). [Serie A](/wiki/Serie_A "Serie A") club [Udinese Calcio](/wiki/Udinese_Calcio "Udinese Calcio") bought his rights in the summer of 2005, from local [Deportivo Cali](/wiki/Deportivo_Cali "Deportivo Cali"), and loaned him immediately to fellow league team [Ascoli Calcio 1898 FC](/wiki/Ascoli_Calcio_1898_FC "Ascoli Calcio 1898 FC") due to the excess of foreign players in the roster;[[2]](#cite_note-2) however, after some problems in the arrival of the transfer, the player missed pre-season training, only joining it a month into [the season](/wiki/2005%E2%80%9306_Serie_A "2005–06 Serie A").

Aguilar returned to Udinese in January 2006, after having never played for Ascoli,[[3]](#cite_note-3) but received few playing opportunities in the following months (two league matches and the [UEFA Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Europa_League "UEFA Europa League") [1–0 loss](/wiki/2005%E2%80%9306_UEFA_Cup "2005–06 UEFA Cup") at [RC Lens](/wiki/RC_Lens "RC Lens"), all incomplete).[[4]](#cite_note-4)

### Spain

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Abel_Aguilar&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Spain")]

For the following three years, Aguilar was also loaned, always in the [Spanish second division](/wiki/Segunda_Divisi%C3%B3n "Segunda División"). He started out in January 2007 at [Xerez CD](/wiki/Xerez_CD "Xerez CD"),[[5]](#cite_note-5) where he remained until the end of the [2007–08 campaign](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_Segunda_Divisi%C3%B3n "2007–08 Segunda División"), always as first choice.[[6]](#cite_note-Choice-6)

In [2008–09](/wiki/2008%E2%80%9309_Segunda_Divisi%C3%B3n "2008–09 Segunda División"), Aguilar represented [Hércules CF](/wiki/H%C3%A9rcules_CF "Hércules CF"),[[7]](#cite_note-7) being a permanent fixture as the [Alicante](/wiki/Alicante "Alicante") side fell three points short of a return to [La Liga](/wiki/La_Liga "La Liga").[[6]](#cite_note-Choice-6) On 24 July 2009 he was again loaned, now to [Real Zaragoza](/wiki/Real_Zaragoza "Real Zaragoza"),[[8]](#cite_note-8) which in turn promoted to the top flight; he made his debut in the competition on 29 August, starting and playing 71 minutes in a 1–0 home win against [CD Tenerife](/wiki/CD_Tenerife "CD Tenerife"),[[9]](#cite_note-9) and scored all his four league goals during the first two months [of competition](/wiki/2009%E2%80%9310_La_Liga "2009–10 La Liga").[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Aguilar was released by Udinese in July 2010, immediately signing a four-year contract with former club Hércules, [returned to the top division](/wiki/2009%E2%80%9310_Segunda_Divisi%C3%B3n "2009–10 Segunda División") after an absence of 13 years.[[11]](#cite_note-11) He appeared in 34 scoreless games during [the season](/wiki/2010%E2%80%9311_La_Liga "2010–11 La Liga"), which ended in relegation.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

Aguilar returned to the Spanish top tier in the [2012–13 campaign](/wiki/2012%E2%80%9313_La_Liga "2012–13 La Liga"), being loaned to [Deportivo de La Coruña](/wiki/Deportivo_de_La_Coru%C3%B1a "Deportivo de La Coruña").[[13]](#cite_note-13) He netted twice in just his second league appearance to help to a 3–3 draw at [Valencia CF](/wiki/Valencia_CF "Valencia CF"),[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15) but his team was again eventually relegated.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

### Toulouse

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Abel_Aguilar&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Toulouse")]

On 22 August 2013, Aguilar agreed to a three-year deal with [Toulouse FC](/wiki/Toulouse_FC "Toulouse FC").[[17]](#cite_note-17) His maiden appearance in [Ligue 1](/wiki/Ligue_1 "Ligue 1") occurred nine days later, putting on an average performance and being [booked](/wiki/Penalty_card#Yellow_card "Penalty card") in a 2–1 away loss to [SC Bastia](/wiki/SC_Bastia "SC Bastia").[[18]](#cite_note-18)

Aguilar spent his [third year](/wiki/2015%E2%80%9316_Ligue_1 "2015–16 Ligue 1") in France on the sidelines, nursing a serious [ankle](/wiki/Ankle "Ankle") injury.[[19]](#cite_note-19) In the very last moments of the 2016 January [transfer window](/wiki/Transfer_window "Transfer window"), he joined [C.F. Os Belenenses](/wiki/C.F._Os_Belenenses "C.F. Os Belenenses") from Portugal.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

### Return home

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Abel_Aguilar&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Return home")]

On 12 June 2016, after 11 years playing in Europe, Aguilar returned to his country and [Deportivo Cali](/wiki/Deportivo_Cali "Deportivo Cali"), signing a three-year contract.[[21]](#cite_note-21) He scored his first goal for the team on 29 July, in the [2–1 victory](/wiki/2016_Copa_Colombia "2016 Copa Colombia") over [América de Cali](/wiki/Am%C3%A9rica_de_Cali "América de Cali") in the [Copa Colombia](/wiki/Copa_Colombia "Copa Colombia").[[22]](#cite_note-22)

### FC Dallas

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Abel_Aguilar&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: FC Dallas")]

On 27 August 2018, Aguilar joined [Major League Soccer](/wiki/Major_League_Soccer "Major League Soccer") side [FC Dallas](/wiki/FC_Dallas "FC Dallas").[[23]](#cite_note-23) At the end of [the season](/wiki/2018_Major_League_Soccer_season "2018 Major League Soccer season"), he was released.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

### Unión Magdalena

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Abel_Aguilar&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Unión Magdalena")]

Aged 33, Aguilar returned to his country in December 2018, agreeing to a deal at [Unión Magdalena](/wiki/Uni%C3%B3n_Magdalena "Unión Magdalena") for [the upcoming season](/wiki/2019_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A_season "2019 Categoría Primera A season").[[25]](#cite_note-25) On 18 February 2020, he announced his retirement.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

## International career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Abel_Aguilar&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: International career")]

Aguilar was [captain](/wiki/Captain_(association_football) "Captain (association football)") of the [Colombia national team](/wiki/Colombia_national_football_team "Colombia national football team") at [under-17](/wiki/Colombia_national_under-17_football_team "Colombia national under-17 football team") and [under-20 levels](/wiki/Colombia_national_under-20_football_team "Colombia national under-20 football team").[[27]](#cite_note-Semana-27) He rose to stardom as leader of the latter side that finished third at the [2003 FIFA World Youth Championship](/wiki/2003_FIFA_World_Youth_Championship "2003 FIFA World Youth Championship").[[28]](#cite_note-28)

Aguilar was then promoted to the senior squad for the [2004 Copa América](/wiki/2004_Copa_Am%C3%A9rica "2004 Copa América") held in [Peru](/wiki/Peru "Peru"). There, he scored two goals, helping his country to the semi-finals.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

Subsequently, Aguilar returned to the under-20 (again as captain), helping them to a first-place finish at the 2005 South American Youth Cup with the consequent qualification for the [World Championship of the category](/wiki/2005_FIFA_World_Youth_Championship "2005 FIFA World Youth Championship") in the [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands"). After helping the national team to three group-stage wins, he could not prevent a [round-of-16](/wiki/Single-elimination_tournament "Single-elimination tournament") 2–1 ousting at the ends of [Argentina](/wiki/Argentina_national_under-20_football_team "Argentina national under-20 football team").[[27]](#cite_note-Semana-27)

Aguilar was selected by [José Pekerman](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Pekerman "José Pekerman") for his [2014 FIFA World Cup](/wiki/2014_FIFA_World_Cup "2014 FIFA World Cup") squad.[[30]](#cite_note-30) He played his first-ever game in [the tournament](/wiki/FIFA_World_Cup "FIFA World Cup") on 14 June, starting in a 3–0 group stage win against [Greece](/wiki/Greece_national_football_team "Greece national football team") which was also his 50th [cap](/wiki/Cap_(sport) "Cap (sport)").[[31]](#cite_note-31)

Aguilar was also picked for the [2018 World Cup](/wiki/2018_FIFA_World_Cup "2018 FIFA World Cup") in Russia.[[32]](#cite_note-32)
